title: A Striking Costa Rican Beach House Pays Tribute to Latin America
description: A builder and an architect collaborated on a family beach compound using local materials and with a patio and a pool at the center.
author: Tim McKeough

title: Winning the Housing Lottery Allowed This Actress to Stay on Broadway
description: An actress struggled to find both work and an affordable apartment in New York. When she got a studio in a lottery, she could turn her focus to the stage.
author: Joanne Kaufman and Graham Dickie

title: My HOA Neighbor Is Breaking the Rules. What Can I Do?
description: There are contracts between property owners, and also between owners and the community.
author: Jill Terreri Ramos

title: Is Long Beach Island’s Audacious Architecture Being Washed Away?
description: The beloved summer getaway off the Jersey Shore has been a playground for modernism. But a new wave of Hamptons-style mansions may steal its identity.
author: Michael Harmon and Ashok Sinha

title: $1 Million Homes in Curaçao
description: Willemstad, the Caribbean island’s capital, offers Caribbean-style villas, modernist homes, and indoor-outdoor specialties.
author: Alison Gregor

title: $825,000 Homes in Nevada, Idaho and North Carolina
description: A midcentury modern house in Las Vegas, a home with river views in Bonners Ferry, Idaho, and a 1950s cottage in Charlotte, N.C.
author: Angela Serratore

title: A Homeowner Built Townhouses in a Jersey City Backyard
description: As YIMBYs and NIMBYs debate where to build housing, a project in Jersey City, N.J. shows how seemingly irreconcilable sides can find common ground.
author: Jane Margolies

title: Filling a Lenox Hill Living Room With Objects That Inspire
description: The designer Josh Greene splurged on a sexy ’70s credenza and found the perfect display for heirloom antiques in his one-bedroom co-op.
author: Tim McKeough and Katherine Marks

title: Trump’s Deportations Are Costing Americans Jobs, Study Finds
description: According to a new study, construction was impacted more than any other industry studied, with American-born workers losing more jobs than immigrants as a result of the deportations.
author: Ronda Kaysen

title: For Sale: Three Marrakesh Riads for Less Than $800,000
description: Riads, the traditional open-air Moroccan homes or palaces, predominate in the Medina, Marrakesh’s walled ‘old city.’
author: Michael Kaminer

title: World Cup Fever Brings More Soccer Pitches to N.Y.C.
description: Companies, government organizations, nonprofits and schools work to make pitches available to play.
author: Alyson Krueger and Graham Dickie

title: Selena Gomez Lists Her Los Angeles Home for $6.5 Million
description: The house was the setting for her HBO show “Selena + Chef.” Plus, J.Lo drops the price on her Beverly Hills mansion, and Sergey Brin lists a rental.
author: Daniela Gorny

title: Willing to Take Risks in a Beach House on Lake Michigan
description: A couple looking for a second home fell for the architecture of one house, but not the interiors. Luckily, they had a “secret weapon.”
author: Tim McKeough

title: For Sale: Four 19th-Century Homes With Turrets
description: These homes feature conical roofs, stained-glass windows, curved walls and, of course, impressive turrets.
author: Daniela Gorny

title: When a Seller Accepts an Offer, Can They Still Look for a Better Deal?
description: Sales contracts are tricky things. Each side of a deal has some leverage — but the seller has more.
author: Jill Terreri Ramos

title: Weekend Getaways for Sale on the Jersey Shore, the East End and in the Hudson Valley
description: This week’s properties are in Manasquan, N.J., and Southold, Southhampton, Accord, and Woodstock, N.Y.
author: Jill P. Capuzzo, Claudia Gryvatz Copquin and Heather Senison

title: A Bedroom Where Leopard-Print Carpeting and Green Walls Do the Talking
description: The husband-and-wife duo behind Wretched Flowers keep their bedroom cozy with bespoke treasures.
author: Sydney Gore and Lauren Lancaster
